items.title: Gulf conflict sends oil prices higher, ripple effects felt across global economy
items.summary: According to expert insights and scholars from the Kleinman Center for Energy Policy and Perry World House, the latest energy price shocks are reshaping economic stability, global development, geopolitics and the pace of the clean energy transition. Rising energy costs are already affecting transportation, food prices, electricity bills, infrastructure spending, and household affordability worldwide, adding pressure across both developed and emerging economies. "High oil prices mean that people pay more at the pump," she said.

items.title: Teacher remanded over alleged sexual misconduct involving student in Besut
items.summary: JERTIH, May 27 — A male teacher has been remanded for two days starting today to assist in the investigation of a sexual misconduct case involving a secondary school student in Besut. Previously, Besut district police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahman said that the teacher, from a secondary school in the Besut district, was arrested around 3 pm yesterday following a police report made by two students. The issue of a male teacher allegedly molesting a male student in Besut went viral after a Facebook account owner posted about it a few days ago.

items.title: Job ad for shepherds goes viral in China exposing labour market strains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chinese farm owner Zuo Xiaoyong was stunned to see his job ad for shepherds to work in the remote and rugged grasslands south of Mongolia becoming the day's top trending social media post. More than 700 people applied for the two positions, including white-collar employees from megacities Shanghai and Chongqing, factory workers across China, and even university graduates. The response to Zuo's late April ad - which drew 59 million views within hours on Weibo, China's version of X, where it generated 21,000 different discussion threads - reveals the growing strains in the country's job market.

items.title: Hong Kong overtakes Switzerland as world's top cross-border wealth hub on China ties
items.summary: ZURICH: Hong Kong has overtaken Switzerland as the top global booking centre for cross-border wealth, a first that is unlikely to be reversed as hubs in Asia grow faster than the European safe-haven, Boston Consulting Group said on Wednesday. Wealth from China and an IPO boom in 2025 helped Hong Kong rise to a US$2.95 trillion offshore behemoth for the world's rich, narrowly surpassing Switzerland's US$2.94 trillion in cross-border wealth, according to BCG's 2026 Global Wealth Report. Cross-border wealth globally grew 8.4 per cent to US$15.7 trillion last year, driven by strong markets and more demand for geographical diversification, and it flowed overwhelmingly to the world's top 10 booking centres, further boosting concentration, BCG added.

items.title: Goldman Sachs lifts S&P 500 year-end target to 8,000 on strong earnings outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Goldman Sachs has raised its 2026 year-end forecast for the S&P 500 index to 8,000 from 7,600, citing continued strength in corporate earnings. "Earnings growth has powered the entire S&P 500 return so far this year, and we expect this dynamic to continue in the coming months," Goldman Sachs said in a note on Tuesday. The brokerage said AI infrastructure beneficiaries are set to drive about half of the index's earnings growth this year, adding that while weak consumer spending and elevated costs pose risks, strong AI investments would offset these pressures.

items.title: 11-month-old baby girl found drowned in pail of soapy water
items.summary: SEREMBAN: An 11-month-old baby girl died after she was believed to have drowned in a pail filled with soapy water at a house along Jalan Sri Permata Ladang here yesterday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Yatim Osman said the infant was found head-down in the pail. "At 7.22pm yesterday, the Seremban district police headquarters received a report regarding the death of an 11-month-old baby girl at a house.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds alert: Sabah, Sarawak brace for severe thunderstorms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — Malaysia’s Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for several areas across East Malaysia, cautioning residents to expect heavy rain, strong winds, and lightning until 6:00 pm today. The warning, released at 3:35 pm, covers a wide section of Sarawak, including the districts of Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Kapit (Song), Miri (Miri and Marudi), and Limbang. In Sabah, the alert applies to the Pedalaman region — specifically Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, and Beaufort — as well as Ranau on the West Coast, Tawau, and parts of Sandakan, encompassing Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran, and Sandakan town.

items.title: Ferrari unveils 'Luce', its first fully electric car, in a tough market
items.summary: ROME: Italian luxury carmaker Ferrari has unveiled its first fully electric model, the Luce, belatedly joining rivals like Porsche and Lamborghini to offer clean-energy driving thrills. But the Luce – Italian for "Light" – has yet to win over investors, with Ferrari shares the worst performers on the Milan stock exchange Tuesday, tumbling six percent following a presentation of the new vehicle in Rome. Elkann then "donated the car's steering wheel to the pontiff," Ferrari said.

items.title: Immigration chief: 77 officers dismissed since 2022 amid crackdown on integrity breaches
items.summary: KAJANG, May 27 — A total of 77 Malaysian Immigration Department officers have been sacked from 2022 to date, out of 994 disciplinary cases resolved by the agency. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said that for this year alone, 23 disciplinary cases have been settled, with eight officers receiving punitive measures in an effort to strengthen integrity and curb misconduct within the department. Zakaria said misconduct included breaches of standard operating procedures, approving matters beyond authority, manipulating counter settings, absenteeism, drug abuse, and smuggling contraband to detainees at depots.

items.title: Baby girl dies after being found face down in pail of soapy water in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 27 — Police have confirmed that an 11-month-old baby girl died yesterday after she was found face down in a pail of soapy water in a house backyard at Jalan Sri Permata Ladang. Seremban police chief ACP Mohd Yatim Osman said the department received a report on the incident at 7.22 pm. He said the victim was taken to a nearby clinic before being referred to Hospital Tuanku Ja’afar, where a medical officer confirmed her death at 4.30 pm.

items.title: Woman killed, five injured in MPV-car crash near Kuala Krai petrol station
items.summary: KUALA KRAI, May 27 — A woman was killed while five others, including two boys, were injured in an accident involving a multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) and a car near a Caltex petrol station in Laloh here this morning. Kuala Krai Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Nik Ahmad Afsham Nik Pa said the victim, Safinaz Razmi, 52, was pronounced dead at the scene by Ministry of Health (MOH) personnel. The road accident involved a Toyota Alphard MPV and a Perodua Bezza car.

items.title: Anwar donates sacrificial cow to Negri Sembilan media club for Aidiladha
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has donated a sacrificial cow to media practitioners in Negri Sembilan to perform the ritual in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidiladha. The event was organised by the Negri Sembilan Media Club (Manis) in collaboration with the Taman Bandar Senawang Kariah Mosque. Manis chairman Muhd Danial Amsyar Arfi expressed his appreciation to the prime minister, saying the contribution reflected deep concern for media practitioners in the state.

items.title: Fahmi thanks Tunku Ismail for support of armed forces
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Communications Minister and government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has expressed appreciation to the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, for reaffirming his unwavering support for the armed forces. Fahmi said matters relating to military affairs, training exercises, and constitutional issues had already been clarified by the National Security Council (NSC). Meanwhile, the armed forces today expressed their gratitude to Tunku Ismail for his unwavering support.

items.title: [UPDATEDS] Azam to meet police next week over Albert Tei's allegations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki is expected to meet Bukit Aman police next week to assist in investigations into allegations made by businessman Albert Tei. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said police had established contact with the individual involved and arrangements were being made to record further statements. Yesterday, Kumar confirmed that Azam would be called up to assist investigations following a police report lodged by Tei on May 13.

items.title: Man found drowned near university not a student or staff
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A man who was found drowned in a lake near a university was neither a student nor a staff member, the university has clarified. Taylor's University confirmed that the man involved in the drowning incident at a lake within the vicinity of its Lakeside Campus was not affiliated with the university as either a student or staff member. The university added that it is fully cooperating with the authorities.

items.title: Frugal and more online: smarter spenders rewrite luxury's China dream
items.summary: BEIJING: When Beijinger Jacqueline Li first heard that one of her favourite luxury department stores in the city was closing, she was shocked — until she reflected on how Chinese spending habits have changed in recent years. So you'll see that demand for luxury goods has fallen," said the international school admissions officer. PANDEMIC HANGOVER Before the pandemic, China's expanding middle class created a wave of first-time luxury buyers, said Lisa Nan, editor at Jing Daily, a publication focused on China's luxury market.

items.title: [UPDATED] 77 Immigration officers dismissed for disciplinary, integrity offences since 2022
items.summary: KAJANG: A total of 77 Immigration Department officers have been dismissed over disciplinary and integrity-related offences since 2022, including eight officers sacked this year alone. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the department had resolved 971 disciplinary cases between 2022 and 2025, with dismissals among the actions taken against errant officers. He said 23 disciplinary cases had been resolved so far this year.

items.title: Fed's Kashkari calls for focus on inflation risk, mum on timing of next rate move
items.summary: TOKYO: The Federal Reserve must focus on containing inflationary risks that appear to be building, although it is "far too soon" to predict when it could next change interest rates, even as markets price in an October increase, Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari said on Wednesday. Kashkari also said the "inflationary shockwave" triggered globally by the Middle East war could persist, a concern that was already feeding into bond markets. It's affecting virtually every economy around the world," he said.

items.title: Supply shock, debt may hurt central bank independence, ex-Fed executive says
items.summary: TOKYO: Pressures on central bank independence are likely to increase as public dissatisfaction over supply shock-driven inflation may tempt politicians to assign blame to central banks, former Federal Reserve executive Donald Kohn said on Wednesday. Falling real incomes would heighten public dissatisfaction and increase the temptation for political leaders to blame central banks, Kohn said in a speech to Japanese, US and European central bank executives attending a Bank of Japan-hosted conference in Tokyo. "Pressures on independence are likely to grow rather than recede," although central bank independence has endured because experience has repeatedly demonstrated its value, he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Public concern over foreigners persists despite 17,313 illegal immigrant arrests, says Immigration DG
items.summary: KAJANG: Public perception that foreigners are "taking over" parts of the country, especially in Kuala Lumpur, is often fuelled by large gatherings during weekends and festive holidays, despite many of them holding valid immigration documents. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said hotspot areas such as KLCC and parts of the capital often see large groups of foreigners gathering during long weekends and festive seasons, leading some Malaysians to assume they are undocumented migrants. He said Malaysia currently has more than three million foreigners with valid immigration documents.

items.title: TNB solar initiative empowers mosques and local communities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The use of solar energy not only symbolises advancements in green technology but reflects values that emphasise environmental stewardship, prudent resource management and community wellbeing. Among the mosques involved are Masjid Kampung Baru Pulau Redang, Masjid Al-Hijrah Tok Bali and Masjid An-Naim Berek 12, as well as Masjid Al-Huda Dungun, Masjid Kampung Dura Kuala Berang and Masjid Al-Amin Teluk Panglima Garang. Meanwhile, Masjid Kampung Dura Kuala Berang received a 17.36 kWp solar system with a 7 kWh battery, potentially generating annual savings of RM12,087.

items.title: Muslims across country celebrate Aidiladha with prayers and takbir
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, joined more than 10,000 congregants at Masjid Putra in Putrajaya. In Selangor , Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah joined more than 15,000 worshippers at Masjid Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah in Shah Alam. He was accompanied by Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, Raja Muda Tengku Amir Shah, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari, and State Secretary Datuk Ahmad Fadzli Ahmad Tajuddin.

items.title: New 500-bed Petaling Jaya Hospital to feature vertical design concept
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The proposed Petaling Jaya Hospital will be constructed as a vertical facility with a capacity of 500 beds. Amirudin said Sungai Buloh Hospital currently has a capacity of 1,037 beds, Hospital Shah Alam has 490 beds, and UMMC has 946 beds. "Cyberjaya Hospital has a capacity of 288 beds, while Tanjong Karang Hospital has 150 beds.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim hosts royal family gathering for Hari Raya Aidiladha at Johor palace
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 27 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, shared a meal with family members and grandchildren in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidiladha celebration at Istana Bukit Serene here today. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, and Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Johor, together with their children, were also in attendance. Also present were Tunku Panglima Johor Tunku Abdul Rahman Sultan Ibrahim, Tunku Putera Johor Tunku Abu Bakar Sultan Ibrahim, as well as Tunku Tun Aminah Sultan Ibrahim and her husband Datuk Dennis Muhammad Abdullah, accompanied by their children.

items.title: Pandan run? Tengku Zafrul says will contest in any seat PKR picks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 —Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz has declared his readiness to contest in any constituency, including Pandan, in the upcoming elections following the resignation of Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. Earlier, PKR Vice-President Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari announced that Tengku Zafrul has been appointed as the party’s monitoring officer for the constituency. This appointment comes after the constituency was “adopted” by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim following Rafizi’s resignation.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul: I'm ready to contest anywhere, but decision is PKR's to make
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz is prepared to contest anywhere, including Pandan, in the upcoming elections following the resignation of Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. Earlier, PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said Tengku Zafrul, who is the prime minister's senior political adviser, has been appointed as the party's monitoring officer for the constituency. This comes after the constituency was "adopted" by Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim following Rafizi's resignation.

items.title: Thai housing demand recovering but outlook clouded by energy shock risks
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's housing market showed recovery signs in the first quarter of 2026, the state house leader said on Wednesday, with transaction volumes rising on government stimulus even as gains in value lagged, underscoring weak purchasing power. * The bank expects a mild contraction in 2026, with transfers seen down 1.1 per cent in volume and 2.3 per cent in value, and new mortgage lending projected to fall 1.6 per cent. * Residential unit transfers rose 11.2 per cent year-on-year in the first quarter, while value increased a more modest 3.1 per cent, highlighting a shift towards lower-priced homes.

items.title: 'Business as usual': Fakes, piracy thrive in Vietnam as US tariff deadline nears
items.summary: HANOI: At a sprawling bazaar on the outskirts of Vietnam's capital, trade in counterfeit goods remains brisk despite government crackdowns and the threat of US tariffs linked to the issue, according to two visits by Reuters journalists to the market. The USTR and Vietnam's foreign ministry did not respond to requests for comment. Washington regards intellectual property violations in Vietnam as serious and harmful to the US economy.

items.title: Shares scale new peaks as markets eye shaky US-Iran truce
items.summary: The dollar held gains from the previous session after Iran said the US had violated a ceasefire. In early trade, pan-regional Euro Stoxx 50 futures were up 0.23 per cent, German DAX futures rose 0.24 per cent, while FTSE futures were down 0.18 per cent. US stock futures, the S&P 500 e-minis, edged up 0.06 per cent.

items.title: Pro-Net expands local EV digital ecosystem efforts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PRO-Net, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Proton Holdings Bhd, is expanding localisation efforts for its electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem through its Proton e.Mas brand, focusing on digital infrastructure, connected mobility services and charging integration. According to Pro-Net, Proton e.Mas is part of a connected vehicle platform serving over 300,000 vehicles, including Proton models. The platform supports features such as remote vehicle control, connected infotainment, charging integration and mobile app services.

items.title: Ex-cop finds true meaning of sacrifice after losing leg in crash
items.summary: For former Penang special branch officer Assistant Superintendent Abd Samad Kasan, the lesson became painfully real after a horrific accident last year claimed his left leg — yet not his spirit to carry on. Instead of surrendering to despair, Samad chose to rise. "Life must go on.

items.title: Hong Kong watchdog raids local units of two Chinese brokerages
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Kong securities regulator has raided the local arms of two major Chinese brokerages as it investigates suspected misconduct tied to share offerings, sources said, the latest move by authorities to ramp up policing of an initial public offering (IPO) boom in the city. The Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) searched the Hong Kong offices of CCB International (CCBI) and China Securities International (CSCI) as part of its probe, said the sources with knowledge of the matter. CCB International and China Securities International are the offshore units of Beijing-headquartered China Construction Bank Corp and China Securities Co , respectively.

items.title: Unduk Ngadau finalist puts spotlight on Dusun Labuk language
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Spectators may get the rare opportunity to hear the Dusun Labuk language at the state-level Kaamatan Unduk Ngadau pageant this Sunday. Dusun Labuk is primarily spoken in Telupid and Beluran along the Labuk River. "For example, the Dusun Labuk word for language is 'tangar', while in the Dusun Liwan dialect it is 'boros'.

items.title: Hengyuan Refining returns to profit with RM525.55mil Q1 gain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hengyuan Refining Company Bhd returned to the black in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), posting a net profit of RM525.55 million from a net loss of RM170.44 million a year ago. Looking ahead, Hengyuan Refining said the downstream refining outlook for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026, will continue to be shaped by global supply and demand dynamics. It added that refining margins are expected to remain sensitive to changes in supply conditions, seasonal demand trends, and developments in key refining facilities and producing regions.

items.title: New PJ hospital site finalised; Selangor MB pledges to expedite approvals for 500-bed facility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The site for the long-awaited Petaling Jaya public hospital has been officially confirmed, paving the way for a new 500-bed facility to serve the rapidly growing population of the Petaling district. Currently, residents in the area rely on Hospital Sungai Buloh, Hospital Shah Alam, and the University Malaya Medical Centre (UMMC). The new hospital is expected to provide much-needed relief for residents in Petaling Jaya, Subang Jaya, and Puchong.

items.title: Fahmi: PKR gains more than 5,000 new members without major campaigns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — PKR recorded more than 5,000 new members between April and this month, said PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Fahmi, who is Lembah Pantai MP, said 10 of the cows were managed at the main site of the Lembah Pantai Korban Perdana Perpaduan programme today. Fahmi said the programme was also attended by representatives from Unity Government component parties, including Lembah Pantai Amanah, UMNO and PKR.

items.title: Two men stabbed at LG Electronics office in Seoul; suspect in custody
items.summary: SEOUL: Two men were stabbed Wednesday at an LG Electronics office in western Seoul, with the suspect currently in custody, authorities said, Yonhap News Agency reported. The suspect fled the scene and was later apprehended near Digital Media City Station, police said. Police said they plan to question the suspect to determine the details.

items.title: Muslims nationwide mark Aidiladha with prayers, royal attendance across states
items.summary: In Selangor, the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, joined more than 15,000 worshippers in performing the Aidiladha prayers at Masjid Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah in Shah Alam. Also present were the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin and the Raja Muda of Selangor Tengku Amir Shah. In Kedah, the Sultan of Kedah Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah performed the Aidiladha prayers at Masjid Zahir in Alor Setar together with more than 2,000 congregants, including Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor and state secretary Datuk Seri Norizan Khazali.

items.title: Former President Biden sues DOJ over release of interview audio
items.summary: UNITED STATES: Former Democratic US President Joe Biden sued the US Department of Justice on Tuesday, seeking to bar the release of audio recordings and transcripts of private conversations with his biographer in 2016 and 2017. The lawsuit, filed in federal court in Washington DC, comes ahead of the department's planned June 15 release of the materials to the US House Judiciary Committee and the conservative Heritage Foundation. The department fought the Heritage Foundation's 2024 request for the records as exempt from the Freedom of Information Act until President Donald Trump took office, the lawsuit claims.

items.title: The PR accreditation debate: Are we solving the right problem?
items.summary: LETTERS: After more than six decades of advocating accreditation for Malaysia's PR industry, perhaps the more important question is not whether accreditation is needed, but why the idea has never truly gained broad industry traction. The call to professionalise the communications industry is valid and timely amid growing concerns over misinformation, AI-driven manipulation, influencer persuasion and declining public trust. The industry certainly needs stronger ethics, accountability and competency standards.

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin to launch new political party in mid-June, source reveals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — A new political party led by Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin is expected to be unveiled in the middle of next month, according to a source aligned with the former Bersatu deputy president. The source, who was also sacked from Bersatu, said preparations for the launch in Kuala Lumpur were “almost complete”, Free Malaysia Today reported. “Yes, Hamzah will announce his new party in mid-June.

items.title: Police officer and wife killed, four children injured in Pekan crash
items.summary: Pekan police chief Supt Mohd Zaidi Mat Zin said the victims were Inspector Syah Lokman Ludza Ramli, 50, who served at the Kuantan District Police Headquarters, and his wife Nurulfiza Ardiana Ambak, 46, both of whom were pronounced dead at the scene. He said the accident is believed to have occurred at about 3 am and involved a Proton Wira driven by the victim and a Honda City. “Initial investigations found that the Honda City, driven by a 26-year-old man, was travelling from Gelang Patah to Terengganu, while the Proton Wira driven by the victim was heading from Kuantan towards Jempol.

items.title: Malaysian man arrested in Bangkok airport over alleged attempt to smuggle 250 wild animals
items.summary: BANGKOK, May 27 — Thai authorities have arrested a Malaysian man at Suvarnabhumi Airport for allegedly attempting to smuggle more than 250 live wild animals to India, in the latest crackdown on transnational wildlife trafficking networks. According to Thai authorities, the suspect was due to travel from Bangkok’s Suvarnabhumi Airport to Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport in Kolkata, India. Authorities said the suspect has been charged under the Wildlife Preservation and Protection Act 2019, the Customs Act 2017, the Animal Epidemics Act 2015 and the Fisheries Royal Ordinance 2015.

items.title: Digital education key to Online Safety Act age verification rollout, says cyber group
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The implementation of an age verification mechanism under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) must be supported by comprehensive digital education efforts to ensure users, especially young people, understand the risks and challenges in cyberspace. Malaysia Cyber Consumer Association (MCCA) president Siraj Jalil said the association supports the government’s move to introduce the mechanism to prevent underage children from owning social media accounts, but its implementation must be accompanied by broader explanations and engagement sessions. “We agree with the age verification mechanism because social media platforms have long set age guidelines, but there has never been a truly effective method to ensure that ages are not falsified.

items.title: Steven Sim says Malaysia’s diversity is key strength in attracting global investors
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 27 — Malaysia’s multicultural society and cultural understanding became key economic advantages in attracting global investors and expanding international business ties, said Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development Minister Steven Sim. Sim said therefore, the country’s diversity should be viewed as a national strength rather than a weakness, particularly in an increasingly competitive global economy. He said that this multicultural advantage complemented the country’s political stability and strategic geographical position amid growing global economic uncertainty.

items.title: Malaysian Armed Forces expresses gratitude to TMJ for ‘unwavering support’ amid military training row
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) has expressed its “highest gratitude” to the Regent of Johor (TMJ), Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, for his public backing of military exercises and his commitment to the welfare of veterans. In a statement today, MAF said the regent’s support reflects a “strong strategic synergy” between the royal institution, the Johor state government, and the military. The military also highlighted the Regent’s assurance that Johor’s doors remain open to MAF veterans.

items.title: Missing hiker Nur Izzati Humaira laid to rest in early morning burial
items.summary: TANJONG MALIM, May 27 — The remains of 19-year-old Nur Izzati Humaira Azizul, the girl who died after going missing while hiking Bukit Changkat Asa last Saturday, were laid to rest at the Felda Trolak Utara Muslim cemetery at 3.05 am today. Earlier, her body was taken to Masjid Ar-Riduaniah Mosque in Felda Trolak Utara for funeral prayers, which were led by her father, Azizul Abdul Aziz, 48, at 2.20 am, joined by around 50 worshippers. Azizul Abdul Aziz, 48, says a prayer after the burial of his daughter, Nur Izzati Humaira Azizul, 18, at the Felda Trolak Utara Muslim cemetery after she died while hiking Bukit Changkat Asa.

items.title: ‘I got the boot’: Subang MP Wong Chen claims abrupt removal from MDV after turnaround
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — Subang MP Wong Chen has revealed his abrupt removal as the non-executive chairman of Malaysia Debt Ventures (MDV), despite leading the government-linked company (GLC) back to profitability during his three-year tenure. The letter from the MoF, dated May 25, 2026, instructed that his chairmanship be terminated effective May 31. “MoF didn't even bother to address the letter to me.

items.title: Fahmi: Putrajaya to examine’s Suhakam’s Taiping Prison findings before taking action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The government will scrutinise Suhakam’s findings on the Taiping Prison incident that left one inmate dead before deciding on further action, says Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. We are concerned about Suhakam’s findings. One detainee, Gan Chin Eng, died following the incident.

items.title: Save MSMEs to prevent ‘magnified’ job losses, Guan Eng urges Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — DAP national advisor Lim Guan Eng has urged Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to prioritise the survival of micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) to prevent a massive wave of unemployment, following a 21 per cent spike in job losses last month. Should MSMEs collapse, the repercussions on job losses will be magnified many fold,” Lim warned. Five proposals for economic relief To mitigate the impact of rising oil prices, supply chain disruptions, and logistics costs, Lim proposed five specific financial and economic measures.

items.title: Anwar reaffirms Malaysia’s stand against West Asia aggression in call with Iran president
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he conveyed this position to Iranian President Dr Masoud Pezeshkian during a telephone conversation yesterday “Alhamdulillah, I had the opportunity to hold a phone conversation with the President of the Islamic Republic of Iran, Dr Masoud Pezeshkian, to extend Eid Mubarak greetings as well as fraternal salutations and prayers from all Malaysians for the people of Iran,” he said in a Facebook post. Anwar said that during the conversation, he also expressed hope that Iran would soon return to peace and that all tensions and bloodshed could be brought to an end through peaceful means and responsible diplomacy. Meanwhile, Anwar said Pezeshkian also expressed appreciation for Malaysia’s consistent stance in upholding peace, justice and humanitarian principles at the international level.

items.title: Ministers urge Muslims to reflect on sacrifice, unity in Aidiladha messages
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — Cabinet Ministers today extended Aidiladha greetings and urged all Muslims to reflect on the true meaning of sacrifice. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail hoped that Muslims would appreciate and apply the sacrifices of Prophet Ibrahim AS and his son, Prophet Ismail AS, in their daily lives. “On this blessed day, let us together appreciate the true meaning of Aidiladha, which demands values of sacrifice, sincerity, gratitude and compassion for one another,” he said.

items.title: Four hurt, including two children, in five-vehicle crash on LPT2 near Paka
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 27 — Four people, including two children, were injured while 14 others escaped unharmed in a five-vehicle accident at Kilometre 337.7 of the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2) near the Paka Rest and Service Area (R&R) here yesterday evening. Paka Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Senior Fire Officer II Mohd Bakri Abdul Rahman said in the 7.30 pm incident, all four victims who were injured were travelling in a Nissan Almera. He added that six, including four children, were travelling in a Toyota Vellfire, while another four, including two children in a Perodua Ativa, were also not injured.

items.title: Malaysia Prisons Department vows transparent review after Suhakam’s report of systemic violence and abuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The Malaysia Prisons Department has pledged to conduct a thorough and transparent review of a Human Rights Commission (Suhakam) report that detailed allegations of systemic violence and medical negligence at Taiping Prison. In a statement issued today, the department said it takes “seriously” every finding and recommendation raised by the Suhakam inquiry panel, including allegations implicating prison management and staff. “The Malaysia Prisons Department will render full cooperation to all relevant parties in examining the report thoroughly, in detail, and with integrity, to ensure that every matter raised receives appropriate attention,” the statement read.

items.title: Anwar, Dr Wan Azizah perform Aidiladha prayers at Masjid Putra with 10,000 congregants
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim performed the Aidiladha sunat prayer at Masjid Putra here today. He arrived with his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, at 7.56 am and was received by the Malaysian Islamic Development Department (Jakim) director-general, Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee, before entering the main hall to perform the prayers. Anwar then recited the takbir Raya alongside more than 10,000 congregants before performing the sunat prayer, which was led by the mosque’s grand Imam, Dr Muhammad Zakuwa Rodzali.

items.title: ‘I have no idea what this fellow is talking about’: Azam Baki denies Albert Tei’s claim of threats and intimidation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has dismissed allegations of criminal intimidation made by businessman Albert Tei as “baseless,” urging the police to instead investigate the latter for lodging a false report. Azam’s rebuttal comes after the police confirmed they will be summoning the former MACC chief to facilitate investigations into Tei’s report. Azam, however, accused Tei of “playing games” and spreading misinformation.

items.title: TMJ chides critics of military exercises, reaffirms Johor’s full support for MAF
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The Regent of Johor (TMJ), Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, has reaffirmed his “unwavering support” for the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF), asserting that the state remains fully open to military exercises essential for national sovereignty. “Military personnel should never be subjected to unnecessary obstacles in carrying out training and operational exercises that are vital to national defence and preparedness,” Tunku Ismail said. NSC pledges strict safety protocols The Regent’s statement comes as the National Security Council (NSC) moved to address concerns regarding the coordination of military drills.

items.title: War on fine print: Bank Negara uses psychology to design financial documents Malaysians might actually read
items.summary: After all, PDS contain important information a consumer should understand before committing to a financial product, from repayment obligations to potential risks. Well, banking regulators like Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) design them using behavioural insights in order to fit that purpose. It’s just science All PDS are now standardised under BNM’s policy document to ensure uniformity and make it easier for consumers to compare offerings across different financial service providers (FSP).

items.title: Thick fog forces overnight delay before helicopter airlifts injured Malaysian hiker from Mount Rinjani
items.summary: Indonesia’s National Search and Rescue Agency (Basarnas) in a statement said the hiker was safely rescued on Tuesday morning after bad weather delayed rescue operations overnight. “Responding to the report received on Monday, rescue personnel from the Kayangan rescue post and the Mataram Search and Rescue (SAR) Office were immediately deployed to the location. To accelerate the evacuation process, a helicopter operated by PT SGi Air Bali was dispatched directly from Bali,” the agency said.

items.title: Durian Tunggal shooting probe resubmitted, police say all AGC directives acted upon
items.summary: KAJANG, May 26 — The investigation paper into a shooting that left three men dead in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, in November last year has been resubmitted to the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC). Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the investigation team had completed several additional directives requested by the prosecution, adding that the paper had already been referred to the AGC four times previously for guidance. “All AGC directives have been acted upon, and the latest investigation paper was submitted yesterday for further direction and advice.
